title: Type Conversions
description: How to change a number into a different kind of number.

slides.title: Stubborn Types 🛑
slides.content:
## Ints don't mix with Floats

Remember how Go is statically typed? It is very strict about it.

If you try to add an `int` (whole number) and a `float64` (decimal) together, Go will complain and refuse to compile. You must make them the *same type* first!

quizData.question: In Go, you can seamlessly add an `int` type to a `float64` type without any extra code.
quizData.explanation: Go prevents hidden errors by forcing you to convert types manually before mixing them.

slides.title: Converting Types 🎭
slides.content:
To convert a value, wrap it in the name of the type you want to change it to.

*   `float64(value)` converts a value into a float64.
*   `int(value)` converts a value into an integer.

Let's see it in action!

quizData.question: If you have `score := 99.9` (float64), how would you convert it to an integer (trimming the decimal)?
quizData.explanation: In Go, conversion uses the formula `Type(value)`. Note that converting a float down to an int deletes everything after the decimal point!

slides.title: Loss of Data 📉
slides.content:
Be careful when converting a floating-point number (like `3.99`) down to an `int`.

Go doesn't round the number up to 4... it aggressively chops off the decimal! `int(3.99)` becomes just `3`.
